sub: UTM — the air traffic control that has no controllers

title: UAS Traffic Management (UTM)
desc: The ecosystem that keeps drones, aircraft and authorities in one picture

body.alt: UTM ecosystem diagram: drone, mobile network, drone detection, GBSAS, radar, UTM and ATM systems

title: Who Talks to Whom in UTM
desc: Reading the ecosystem diagram as a data-flow problem

rows: Mobile network → UTM; command & control back to the operator
UTM — receives clearance and conflict warnings in return
DFS, authorities, pilots — and the ATM system

items: UTM is not a tower with people — it is a federation of data services
Every arrow in the diagram is an interface: this is why Modules 6–10 are about APIs, streaming, messaging and testing

title: Automated Fleet Control
desc: Operating one drone is a hobby — operating five hundred is a systems problem

body.card: What a fleet control system does
items: Mission planning — routes computed against geo-zones, weather, battery range and payload
Geofencing — hard boundaries enforced on board and monitored on the ground
Telemetry ingestion — position, battery, health of every vehicle, continuously
Tasking & scheduling — assigning vehicles to missions, handling battery swaps and hand-overs
Contingency management — lost link, low battery, weather change: pre-planned responses, not improvisation
One operator supervises many drones — the software must escalate only what needs a human
Everything else is automation: exactly the 'efficient control & monitoring' this module is named after

title: Sensor Fusion for Precise Control
desc: No single sensor is good enough — the mix is the magic

rows: Jamming, urban canyons, GPS-unreliable zones (remember the NOTAM!)
Position relative to terrain, obstacle detection

items: A Kalman-filter (or similar estimator) fuses all inputs into one consistent state estimate
Fusion buys redundancy: when GNSS degrades, vision + IMU carry the drone — a direct application of Module 3's safety thinking
